response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and backend web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is absent from his resume entirely. His primary technical stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) has no overlap with the required C/UNIX embedded development, Linux kernel modification, or LAN-switch security domains such as MACsec, 802.1X, or Captive Portal authentication. While he briefly touched C/C++ at National Instruments over a decade ago, there is no evidence of sustained embedded or networking security work.
strengths: Extensive overall software engineering career (15+ years) spanning web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Experience with Linux environments and low-latency systems (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic, Sound Position Server thesis using Linux/C++)
Demonstrated ability to work across the stack and pick up new technologies quickly across multiple domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — this is a hard, non-negotiable requirement for the role
No experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC auth, encryption key exchange, or network switch development)
C programming is peripheral and dated (brief NI stint in 2014–2015); primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js with no Linux kernel development experience
